wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Mela project overview section focused on the campus event discovery platform. Explain its purpose as a centralized web platform connecting students, organizers, and moderators across Lahore's academic institutions. Document the core problem it solves, key features for different user roles (students, organizers, moderators), technology stack overview, and project architecture. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the platform's value proposition and user workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Mela UI component system. Detail the design system architecture including reusable components (Button, Card, Input, Badge) with their props, styling patterns, and usage guidelines. Explain the Brutalist design principles implementation, component composition patterns, and how components handle state and events. Document component APIs, customization options, and integration with the overall design language. Include practical examples of component usage across different pages and scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Mela authentication service. Document the complete authentication flow including email/password registration, Google OAuth login, and session management. Explain the service methods signUp(), signIn(), signInWithGoogle(), logOut(), onAuthChange(), getCurrentUser(), and getCurrentUserProfile(). Detail parameter requirements, return value structures, error handling patterns, and integration with Firebase Authentication and Firestore. Include practical examples of authentication state management, user profile creation, and role-based access control. Address security considerations, token handling, and best practices for authentication in React applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Firebase Cloud Functions implementation in Mela. Detail the event approval and rejection workflows, moderator verification system, user profile creation triggers, and scheduled operations. Document the onCall functions including approveEvent, rejectEvent, and checkModeratorStatus with their parameters, authentication requirements, and error handling. Explain the auth trigger for automatic user profile creation and the pubsub scheduled functions for event reminders and cleanup operations. Include function deployment procedures, testing strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address security considerations, logging practices, and monitoring approaches for cloud functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Mela platform focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Document prerequisites including Node.js 18+, Firebase account requirements, and Firebase CLI installation. Provide step-by-step instructions for cloning the repository, setting up frontend and backend environments, configuring Firebase services, and deploying the application. Include environment variable configuration, local development server startup, and initial testing procedures. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for first-time users. Make the content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Feed Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Event Feed page component. Document the event browsing interface including search functionality, filtering by university and category, and upcoming events toggle. Explain the state management for loading states, search terms, and filter configurations. Detail the integration with eventService for fetching filtered and searched events. Include the card-based layout implementation, category-based styling with color mapping, and responsive design patterns. Document the user interaction flows for searching, filtering, and navigating to event details.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Discovery and Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the event discovery and filtering system. Document the implementation of university-specific filtering, category-based sorting, and date-based searches using Firestore queries. Explain the getFilteredEvents function and its constraint-based approach to building dynamic queries. Cover the searchEvents implementation with its limitations and potential improvements. Include examples of filter combinations, performance considerations for large datasets, and real-time filtering capabilities. Document the integration between service layer queries and UI components for seamless user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Collections Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Mela's Firestore collections overview. Detail the five main collections: users, events, submissions, savedEvents, and optional moderators collection. Explain the purpose and scope of each collection, their relationships, and how they work together to support the application's functionality. Document collection-level data models, primary keys, and collection-specific business rules. Include examples of typical documents in each collection and explain the rationale behind the collection design decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP Callable Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the HTTP callable functions in Mela's Cloud Functions. Document the approveEvent function with its parameters (submissionId), authentication requirements, university authorization checks, and the complete approval workflow including Firestore operations for moving documents from submissions to events collection. Detail the rejectEvent function with submissionId and reason parameters, rejection logic, and status updates. Explain the checkModeratorStatus function that returns user role and university permissions. Include function signatures, parameter validation, error handling patterns, authentication middleware (requireAuth), and utility functions like isModeratorFor. Provide practical examples of function calls, expected responses, and error scenarios. Address security considerations, input sanitization, and performance optimization for Firestore operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Collection Schemas
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Firestore collection schemas in Mela. Detail each collection structure including users, events, submissions, and savedEvents with complete field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business logic constraints. Document the schema design principles behind each collection, field relationships, and data normalization strategies. Include example documents for each collection showing typical use cases. Explain the rationale for denormalized fields like eventTitle and eventDateTime in savedEvents collection. Address collection-specific constraints, optional vs required fields, and data validation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for student-facing features in the Mela platform. Detail the event browsing interface including the event feed component, filtering mechanisms by university and category, search functionality by title and description, and pagination handling. Document the event details view showing rich information display, poster image handling, and event metadata presentation. Explain the event saving system including bookmark functionality, reminder setup, and saved events management. Cover user profile integration, university selection, and preference management. Include practical examples of common student workflows, component usage patterns, and integration with the service layer. Address performance considerations for large event datasets and real-time updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Mela development environment. Cover prerequisites including Node.js 18+, Firebase account setup, and Firebase CLI installation. Document step-by-step local development setup for both frontend and backend, including cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and running the development server. Explain Firebase project configuration, authentication setup, and initial deployment procedures. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper environment configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Mela page components architecture. Document each route-based page component including LandingPage, EventFeed, EventDetails, SubmitEvent, ModeratorDashboard, Login, Profile, MySubmissions, and SavedEvents. Explain the page composition patterns, data fetching strategies, user interaction flows, and integration with the service layer. Include component lifecycle management, state handling, and how pages coordinate with Firebase services for real-time updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Management Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Mela event management service. Document all event-related operations including event retrieval, filtering, searching, and real-time updates. Explain service methods for fetching upcoming events, popular events, university-specific events, and event details. Detail query parameters, filtering options, pagination support, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of event data structures, search algorithms, and caching strategies. Address Firestore query patterns, indexing requirements, and real-time listener management for efficient event updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Firestore database design in Mela. Detail the collections structure including users, events, submissions, and savedEvents with their field definitions, data types, and relationships. Document the database schema design principles, indexing strategy for query optimization, and security rule implementation for access control. Explain the data flow between collections, relationship patterns, and data lifecycle management. Include performance considerations, query optimization techniques, and scalability planning. Address data validation rules, business logic constraints, and audit trail requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for the Mela platform covering frontend, backend, and development tools. Document React 19 as the primary UI framework, React Router for navigation, Vite as the build tool, and vanilla CSS styling approach. Cover Firebase ecosystem integration including Firestore database, Authentication services, Cloud Functions, Storage, and Hosting. Explain the Brutalist design system philosophy and color palette implementation. Detail development dependencies, build configuration, and deployment pipeline. Include version compatibility requirements, third-party integrations, and architectural rationale for technology choices. Provide comparison with alternative solutions and justification for the selected stack.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Submission Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Event Submission page component. Document the form-based event creation interface including input validation, image upload functionality, and submission workflow. Explain the integration with submissionService for handling event data processing and approval workflows. Detail the form state management, error handling, and success feedback mechanisms. Include the step-by-step submission process, required field validations, and user guidance patterns. Document the relationship with cloud functions for backend processing and the approval/rejection workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Submission and Approval Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the event submission and approval workflow. Detail the submission form interface, image upload handling, and validation processes. Explain the transition from submissions collection to approved events, including the approval/rejection mechanisms. Document the submissionService integration, form state management, and error handling strategies. Cover the organizer role requirements, submission tracking, and status updates. Include examples of submission forms, image processing workflows, and moderation integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Users Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the users collection schema and implementation. Document all user profile fields including email, displayName, photoURL, university, role, and moderatorFor arrays. Explain the role-based access control system with student, moderator, and admin roles. Detail the university-based moderation assignment mechanism and how moderators are linked to specific universities. Include field validation rules, data types, and constraints. Provide examples of different user profiles and explain the relationship between user roles and access permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Triggers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Firebase Authentication trigger functions in Mela. Detail the createUserProfile function that automatically creates user profiles when new users sign up through Firebase Authentication. Explain the trigger mechanism, user data extraction from Firebase Auth context, and Firestore document creation process. Document the user profile structure including email, displayName, photoURL, university, role (default student), moderatorFor array, and timestamp fields. Include the automatic role assignment logic, university field initialization, and error handling patterns. Provide examples of user registration flow, profile creation timing, and potential failure scenarios. Address security considerations for user data handling and Firestore security rules implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Firestore indexing strategy in Mela. Document all composite indexes required for optimal query performance including the specific index configurations for events collection (university+dateTime, category+dateTime, university+category+dateTime), submissions collection (university+status+createdAt), and savedEvents collection (userId+eventDateTime, reminderSent+eventDateTime). Explain the query patterns that require these indexes, performance implications of missing indexes, and best practices for index maintenance. Include guidance on when to add new indexes based on query requirements and how to optimize existing indexes for better performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Organizer Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for event organizer capabilities in the Mela platform. Document the event submission form including rich text editor integration, poster image upload with validation, required field validation, and form state management. Explain the submission tracking system showing status indicators (pending, approved, rejected), timeline visualization, and rejection feedback display. Detail the submission editing functionality allowing organizers to modify pending submissions before approval. Cover the submission history view with filtering and sorting capabilities. Include practical examples of the complete submission workflow, error handling for validation failures, and integration with Firebase Storage for image uploads. Address form validation patterns, user feedback mechanisms, and submission lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Mela frontend system. Describe the React 19 application structure, component hierarchy, and design system implementation. Document the component architecture including reusable UI components (Button, Card, Input, Badge), page components organization, and routing configuration. Explain the styling approach using Vanilla CSS with Brutalist design principles. Include component composition patterns, state management strategies, and integration with Firebase services. Address the modular structure and how different parts of the frontend communicate.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Mela routing and navigation system. Explain the React Router configuration, route definitions, and navigation patterns. Document the Navbar component implementation, active link handling, and responsive navigation behavior. Include route guards, navigation state management, and how different user roles access specific routes. Address the relationship between routing and authentication, as well as programmatic navigation patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Submission Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Mela event submission service. Document the complete submission workflow including event creation, image upload processing, status tracking, and submission management. Explain service methods for creating submissions, uploading event posters, updating submission status, and retrieving submission history. Detail file upload handling, validation rules, approval workflows, and error recovery mechanisms. Include examples of submission data models, progress tracking, and integration with cloud storage. Address submission lifecycle management, moderation integration, and user feedback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Firebase deployment and configuration management in Mela. Document the firebase.json configuration including hosting settings, functions configuration, and database rules deployment. Explain the deployment pipeline for cloud functions, static hosting, and database schema updates. Detail environment variable management, CI/CD integration, and rollback procedures. Include Qodo AI agents and workflows configuration for automated tasks. Address monitoring setup, logging configuration, and performance monitoring. Document backup procedures, disaster recovery planning, and maintenance schedules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive feature overview documentation for the Mela platform covering functionality for all user roles. Document student features including event browsing, filtering by university and category, search capabilities, event saving with reminders, and detailed event viewing. Cover organizer features such as event submission with rich details and poster images, status tracking for submissions, editing pending submissions, and viewing rejection feedback. Detail moderator capabilities including submission review, approval/rejection with feedback, approved event editing, and university-specific moderation management. Include user role hierarchy, permission systems, and workflow automation. Provide practical examples of each feature in action and explain the value proposition for different stakeholder groups.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Details Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Event Details page component. Document the event information display interface including event metadata, description formatting, and action buttons for saving events. Explain the integration with eventService for retrieving detailed event information and managing user interactions. Detail the bookmark/save functionality, event sharing capabilities, and related event recommendations. Include the responsive layout implementation, image display handling, and user engagement features. Document the real-time updates and state synchronization with the event feed.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Display and Details
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for event display and details functionality. Document the getEventById function and its integration with the EventDetails page component. Explain event data formatting, timestamp conversion, and display formatting for dates and times. Cover the relationship between event listing and individual event views, including navigation patterns and data loading strategies. Document event sharing capabilities, social features, and user interaction elements. Include examples of event detail layouts, responsive design considerations, and accessibility features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Events Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the events collection structure and approved event data model. Document all event fields including title, description, dateTime, university, category, venue, posterURL, and organizer information. Explain the approved event lifecycle and how approved events differ from submissions. Detail the denormalized fields (eventTitle, eventDateTime) used for efficient querying and display. Include event categorization system, university filtering capabilities, and the relationship between events and their organizers. Provide examples of approved events and explain the approval process workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scheduled Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the scheduled Cloud Functions in Mela. Detail the sendEventReminders function that runs every hour to find saved events happening within the next 24 hours and marks them for reminder processing. Explain the cleanupRejectedSubmissions function that runs daily to remove rejected submissions older than 30 days. Document the scheduling syntax, cron expressions, and execution timing. Include the Firestore query logic for finding eligible events, batch update operations for marking reminders, and batch delete operations for cleanup. Provide examples of scheduled function configuration, monitoring approaches, and potential optimization strategies. Address performance considerations for large datasets, error handling for failed executions, and logging practices for scheduled operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firestore security rules implementation in Mela. Detail the access control logic for each collection including user profile access permissions, event visibility rules, submission review workflows, and saved events management. Document the role-based access control system with student, moderator, and admin permissions. Explain the security rule expressions for university-specific access, moderator approval workflows, and data integrity constraints. Include examples of common access scenarios, rule testing approaches, and debugging techniques for security rule issues. Address performance considerations and best practices for maintaining secure and efficient access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Moderator Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for moderator capabilities in the Mela platform. Detail the submission review interface including pending submission lists, approval/rejection workflows, and feedback system for rejections. Document the approved event management functionality for editing existing events and maintaining quality standards. Explain university-specific moderation controls including assignment verification, scope limitations, and jurisdiction management. Cover the moderator dashboard features such as submission statistics, review analytics, and bulk operation capabilities. Include practical examples of moderation workflows, decision-making processes, and integration with Firebase Cloud Functions for automated moderation tasks. Address role-based access controls, security considerations, and audit trail functionality for moderation actions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Mela service layer architecture. Explain the service abstraction pattern used to encapsulate Firebase operations, error handling strategies, and authentication service implementation. Document each service module including authService for user authentication, eventService for event management, submissionService for event submissions, userService for profile management, and moderatorService for administrative functions. Include service method signatures, parameter requirements, return value structures, and integration patterns with Firebase SDK. Address reusability, testing strategies, and maintenance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Mela state management architecture. Explain how React hooks are used for local component state, how Firebase real-time listeners manage global state, and the integration between local and remote state. Document the authentication state flow, event data state management, and user session handling. Include patterns for state synchronization with Firestore, error state management, and loading state handling across different components and pages.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Mela user profile service. Document user profile management operations including profile retrieval, updates, saved events management, and reminder settings. Explain service methods for updating user information, managing saved events, setting up notifications, and profile preferences. Detail Firestore operations for user data persistence, bookmarking functionality, and personalization features. Include examples of user data models, profile validation, and integration with authentication service. Address privacy controls, data protection, and user preference management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Mela platform describing the high-level system design and component interactions. Document the React frontend architecture with component hierarchy, routing configuration, and state management patterns. Explain the Firebase backend integration including Cloud Functions deployment, Firestore data modeling, and real-time synchronization. Detail the authentication flow using Firebase Auth and user role-based access control. Cover the design system approach with Brutalist aesthetics and component composition patterns. Include system boundaries, data flow patterns, and integration points between frontend and backend services. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and deployment topology. Document the monorepo structure and separation of concerns between frontend and backend components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Login Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Login page component. Document the authentication interface including email/password login and Google OAuth integration. Explain the authentication flow, form validation, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the integration with authService for managing user sessions and role-based access control. Include the user experience patterns for login states, loading indicators, and success/error feedback. Document the session persistence, redirect handling after authentication, and integration with Firebase Authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Upcoming Events System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the upcoming events system. Explain the getUpcomingEvents function implementation using Firestore timestamp comparisons and ascending order sorting. Document the integration with the main event feed and dedicated upcoming events view. Cover time-based filtering logic, real-time updates for changing event statuses, and performance optimization for time-sensitive queries. Include examples of upcoming event displays, automatic status updates when events expire, and integration with notification systems for event reminders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Submissions Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the submissions collection and event submission workflow. Document the submission lifecycle from initial creation through approval or rejection. Detail all submission fields including status tracking (pending, rejected), rejection reasons, and moderator actions. Explain the relationship between submissions and their associated posters in Firebase Storage. Include the submission review process, moderator responsibilities, and how submissions transition to approved events. Provide examples of different submission states and explain the data consistency mechanisms during the approval process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data flow patterns and lifecycle management in Mela's Firestore implementation. Document the complete data journey from event submission through approval/rejection to final storage, including the transition from submissions to events collection. Explain the reminder system implementation using savedEvents collection, data denormalization strategies, and audit trail requirements. Detail the poster image storage workflow, URL management, and cleanup procedures. Include data consistency patterns, transaction usage, and conflict resolution strategies. Address data archiving, soft delete implementations, and historical data preservation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the user role hierarchy and permission system in Mela. Document the three-tier role structure: Student (default), Moderator (reviewer), and Admin (full system access). Explain role-based access control implementation in Firestore security rules, including university-specific moderation permissions and scope limitations. Detail the user registration and role assignment process, manual moderator creation procedures, and admin privilege management. Cover authentication flows for different user types, session management, and permission verification throughout the application. Include practical examples of role-based UI rendering, feature gating, and security rule patterns. Address role transition workflows, permission inheritance, and security best practices for maintaining role integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Mela's page component system. Document each route-based page component including EventFeed for event browsing, SubmitEvent for event creation, EventDetails for event information display, Login for authentication, Profile for user management, ModeratorDashboard for administrative tasks, MySubmissions for organizer tracking, SavedEvents for bookmarking, and LandingPage for initial user onboarding. Explain page-specific logic, component composition patterns, data fetching strategies, and user interaction flows. Include prop requirements, state management approaches, and integration with service layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Moderation Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Mela moderation service. Document moderator-specific operations including submission review, approval/rejection workflows, university-specific moderation, and audit trail management. Explain service methods for viewing pending submissions, approving/rejecting events, managing moderator permissions, and generating moderation reports. Detail approval logic, rejection reasons, notification systems, and moderation analytics. Include examples of moderation workflows, role-based access control, and integration with submission service. Address moderation best practices, quality assurance processes, and administrative oversight features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Roadmap & Future Plans
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive roadmap documentation for the Mela platform covering current phase achievements and future development plans. Document Phase 1 completion including core event discovery and submission, moderator approval system, and basic filtering capabilities. Detail Phase 2 objectives for multi-department expansion, enhanced search with Algolia integration, and advanced filtering features. Cover Phase 3 vision including mobile app development with React Native, push notifications implementation, event analytics, user recommendations, and expansion beyond university events. Include timeline expectations, resource requirements, and technical challenges for each phase. Provide prioritization criteria for feature development and explain the strategic direction of the platform evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Profile Management page component. Document the user profile display and editing interface including personal information management, university selection, and notification preferences. Explain the integration with userService for profile data retrieval and updates. Detail the form validation, image upload for profile pictures, and preference management. Include the user settings interface, account management features, and integration with authentication state. Document the real-time profile updates and data synchronization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Saved Events Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the savedEvents collection and user bookmarking system. Document the saved events data model including userId references, eventId relationships, and denormalized event information. Explain the dual storage approach with both standalone collection and subcollection alternatives. Detail the reminder system integration and how savedEvents supports user event tracking and notifications. Include the relationship between saved events and the reminderSent flag, and explain how the system prevents duplicate saves and maintains data consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Mela backend system built on Firebase. Describe the cloud functions implementation, Firestore database design, security rules, and storage configuration. Document the business logic implementation including event approval/rejection workflows, user profile management, and scheduled operations. Explain the database schema design with collections for users, events, submissions, and savedEvents. Include security rule implementation, indexing strategy for performance optimization, and deployment procedures. Address scalability considerations, monitoring approaches, and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Moderator Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Moderator Dashboard page component. Document the administrative interface for reviewing event submissions, managing approvals, and overseeing university-specific content. Explain the integration with moderatorService for handling moderation workflows and approval processes. Detail the submission review interface, bulk actions, and audit trail functionality. Include the university-specific moderation features, reporting capabilities, and content management tools. Document the role-based access control and administrative user experience patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexes Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for Firestore indexing strategy and query optimization. Detail the composite indexes required for efficient querying across university, category, and dateTime fields. Explain the indexing patterns for events collection (university+dateTime, category+dateTime, university+category+dateTime), submissions collection (university+status+createdAt), and savedEvents collection (userId+eventDateTime, reminderSent+eventDateTime). Include performance implications of different query patterns, index maintenance costs, and best practices for optimizing Firestore queries. Provide examples of optimized queries and explain the trade-offs between read and write performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Mela's Firestore database schema. Detail entity relationships between users, events, submissions, and savedEvents collections. Document field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints. Explain indexing strategy for efficient querying, composite indexes for filtering operations, and performance optimization techniques. Include data access patterns, query optimization strategies, and security rule implementation. Document data lifecycle management, backup procedures, and schema evolution practices. Address data integrity, privacy considerations, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: My Submissions Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the My Submissions page component. Document the organizer's view of their submitted events including status tracking, edit functionality, and submission history. Explain the integration with submissionService for retrieving user-specific submission data and managing submission states. Detail the submission status indicators, approval/rejection tracking, and resubmission capabilities. Include the user workflow for viewing submission history, understanding rejection reasons, and updating submitted information. Document the real-time status updates and notification systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Rules
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Firestore security rules implementation and access control mechanisms. Document the role-based access control system with specific rules for each collection. Explain user authentication requirements, university-based access restrictions, and moderator privileges. Detail the security rules for read/write operations across users, events, submissions, and savedEvents collections. Include examples of permission scenarios, edge cases in access control, and best practices for maintaining security as the application evolves. Address common security pitfalls and provide guidelines for extending the security model.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Mela's authentication system. Explain user registration and login flows using Firebase Authentication with email/password and Google OAuth options. Document role-based access control implementation with student, moderator, and admin roles. Cover session management, token handling, and user profile creation processes. Include authentication state management, protected route implementation, and security considerations. Document the integration between Firebase Authentication and custom user roles stored in Firestore. Address user experience patterns, error handling, and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Saved Events Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Saved Events page component. Document the user's bookmarked events interface including saved event display, removal functionality, and event management. Explain the integration with userService for retrieving and managing user's saved events. Detail the bookmarking system, event organization features, and reminder notifications. Include the user workflow for managing saved events, setting reminders, and accessing favorite events. Document the real-time synchronization between saved events and user preferences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Mela's event management system. Document event discovery and filtering capabilities including university-specific filtering, category-based sorting, and date-based searches. Explain the event submission process for organizers, image upload handling, and status tracking workflows. Cover event details display functionality, upcoming events logic, and search implementation. Include event CRUD operations, approval workflows, and moderation processes. Document the integration between frontend components and backend services, real-time updates, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Landing Page
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Landing Page component. Document the initial user onboarding interface including platform introduction, navigation guidance, and call-to-action elements. Explain the component structure, responsive design implementation, and user engagement patterns. Detail the navigation elements, feature highlights, and user journey facilitation. Include the visual design elements, branding consistency, and first-time user experience optimization. Document the integration with authentication flows and navigation to key platform features.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile and Bookmarking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for user profile management and bookmarking system. Document profile creation, editing, and management features including personal information, preferences, and university affiliation. Explain the saved events system for bookmarking and reminder functionality. Cover user preference management, notification settings, and profile visibility controls. Include integration with authentication system, data synchronization patterns, and real-time updates. Document the user interface patterns for profile editing, bookmark management, and reminder configuration. Address privacy considerations and data protection measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Mela's build and deployment processes. Document Vite build configuration, environment variable management, and production optimization strategies. Explain Firebase deployment procedures including hosting, functions, Firestore rules, and storage deployment. Cover CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and deployment verification processes. Include environment-specific configurations, rollback procedures, and monitoring setup. Document troubleshooting common deployment issues, performance optimization techniques, and maintenance procedures for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for Mela project contributors. Document code style standards, ESLint configuration, and best practices for React and JavaScript development. Explain component development patterns, service layer implementation guidelines, and testing strategies. Cover the Qodo AI agents and workflows integration for automation. Include contribution workflows, code review processes, and documentation standards. Document debugging techniques, performance profiling, and development tooling. Address team collaboration practices, version control workflows, and project maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and maintenance documentation for Mela. Document common issues encountered during development and production, including Firebase configuration problems, authentication failures, and deployment errors. Explain monitoring approaches using Firebase Console, function logging, and performance tracking. Cover maintenance procedures including database cleanup, security updates, and performance optimization. Include debugging techniques for frontend and backend issues, log analysis strategies, and error resolution workflows. Document preventive maintenance practices, backup procedures, and disaster recovery planning.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Mela - Campus Event Discovery Platform

## Project Introduction

Mela is a centralized web platform designed to solve the problem of fragmented event communication in universities across Lahore. The platform serves three distinct user groups:

- **Students**: Discover events happening across multiple universities
- **Event Organizers**: Submit events for review and reach a wider audience  
- **Moderators**: Verify and approve event submissions to maintain quality

The platform provides a unified solution for campus event discovery, featuring rich event details, filtering capabilities, and a streamlined approval workflow.

## Technical Architecture

Mela follows a modern full-stack architecture built on Firebase's serverless infrastructure:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[Firebase Authentication]
    A --> C[Firebase Firestore]
    A --> D[Firebase Storage]
    A --> E[Firebase Cloud Functions]
    
    F[Moderator Dashboard] --> E
    G[Event Submission] --> E
    H[User Management] --> E
    
    C --> I[Events Collection]
    C --> J[Submissions Collection]
    C --> K[Users Collection]
    C --> L[Saved Events Collection]
    
    D --> M[Event Posters]
    
    E --> N[Approval Functions]
    E --> O[User Profile Creation]
    E --> P[Reminder System]
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured as a monorepo with clear separation between frontend and backend:

**Frontend Architecture:**
- React 18 with React Router for navigation
- Vite for build tooling and development server
- Firebase SDK for real-time database operations
- Custom Brutalist design system with hand-drawn aesthetics

**Backend Architecture:**
- Firebase Cloud Functions for business logic
- Firestore for data persistence
- Firebase Authentication for user management
- Firebase Storage for image hosting

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Services encapsulate Firestore operations
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component rendering based on user roles
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time state management through Firebase listeners
- **Strategy Pattern**: Modular authentication strategies (Email/Password, Google OAuth)

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point:**
- `frontend/src/main.jsx` - React application bootstrap
- `frontend/src/App.jsx` - Routing configuration and layout

**Backend Entry Point:**
- `backend/functions/index.js` - Cloud Functions implementation

### Core Modules

**Service Layer:**
- `frontend/src/services/` - Firebase service abstractions
- `frontend/src/utils/constants.js` - Application-wide constants and utilities

**UI Components:**
- `frontend/src/components/ui/` - Reusable design system components
- `frontend/src/pages/` - Route-level page components

**Firebase Integration:**
- `frontend/src/firebase.js` - Firebase SDK initialization
- `backend/FIRESTORE_SCHEMA.md` - Database schema documentation

### Configuration Approach

**Frontend Configuration:**
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[package.json] --> B[Vite Config]
    A --> C[ESLint Config]
    D[vite.config.js] --> E[Build Settings]
    F[firebase.js] --> G[Environment Variables]
```

**Backend Configuration:**
- `backend/functions/package.json` - Node.js runtime configuration
- `backend/firebase.json` - Firebase project configuration
- `backend/firestore.indexes.json` - Database indexing strategy

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- React 18.2.0 - Core UI framework
- react-router-dom 6.22.2 - Client-side routing
- firebase 10.8.1 - Firebase SDK
- @supabase/supabase-js 2.39.8 - Alternative authentication option

**Backend Dependencies:**
- firebase-admin 12.0.0 - Server-side Firebase operations
- firebase-functions 5.0.0 - Cloud Functions framework

### Integration Points

**Authentication Flow:**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant A as Auth Service
    participant F as Firebase Auth
    participant DB as Firestore
    
    U->>A: Login Request
    A->>F: Authenticate
    F-->>A: Auth Token
    A->>DB: Create User Profile
    DB-->>A: Profile Created
    A-->>U: Session Established
```

**Event Approval Workflow:**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending: New Submission
    Pending --> Approved: Moderator Approval
    Pending --> Rejected: Moderator Rejection
    Approved --> [*]: Event Published
    Rejected --> [*]: Submission Closed
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Event Discovery:**
- Real-time event feed with filtering and search capabilities
- Category-based categorization and university-specific views
- Upcoming events prioritization

**Submission Management:**
- Rich event submission form with image uploads
- Status tracking for submissions (pending, approved, rejected)
- Edit functionality for pending submissions

**Moderation System:**
- Role-based access control with university-specific permissions
- Approval/rejection workflows with feedback mechanisms
- Audit trail through timestamped operations

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Data Flow:**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Action] --> B[Service Call]
    B --> C[Firebase Firestore]
    C --> D[Cloud Functions]
    D --> E[Data Processing]
    E --> F[Collection Update]
    F --> G[Real-time Sync]
    G --> H[UI Update]
```

**Component Relationship Diagram:**
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Navbar] --> B[Auth Service]
    C[EventFeed] --> D[Event Service]
    D --> E[Firestore]
    F[SubmitEvent] --> G[Submission Service]
    G --> E
    H[ModeratorDashboard] --> I[Moderator Service]
    I --> E
```

**Feature Architecture:**
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Event {
        +title: string
        +description: string
        +dateTime: timestamp
        +university: string
        +category: string
        +venue: string
        +posterURL: string
    }
    
    class User {
        +email: string
        +displayName: string
        +role: string
        +university: string
    }
    
    class Submission {
        +status: string
        +rejectionReason: string
        +organizerId: string
    }
    
    class SavedEvent {
        +userId: string
        +eventId: string
        +reminderSent: boolean
    }
    
    Event <|-- Submission
    User --> Event : organizes
    User --> SavedEvent : saves
    Event --> SavedEvent : bookmarked
```

The Mela platform demonstrates a well-architected solution for campus event management, leveraging Firebase's serverless capabilities to provide a scalable, real-time experience for university communities. The modular design enables easy extension and maintenance while the clear separation of concerns ensures long-term sustainability.

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [App.jsx](frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [main.jsx](frontend/src/main.jsx)
- [firebase.js](frontend/src/firebase.js)
- [index.js](backend/functions/index.js)
- [EventFeed.jsx](frontend/src/pages/EventFeed.jsx)
- [Navbar.jsx](frontend/src/components/Navbar.jsx)
- [constants.js](frontend/src/utils/constants.js)
- [eventService.js](frontend/src/services/eventService.js)
- [FIRESTORE_SCHEMA.md](backend/FIRESTORE_SCHEMA.md)

</blog>
